verses.1: The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz, which he saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.
verses.5: Why should you be beaten more,
verses.6: From the sole of the foot even to the head there is no soundness in it,
verses.8: The daughter of Zion is left like a shelter in a vineyard,
verses.9: Unless the LORD of Armies had left to us a very small remnant,
verses.10: Hear the LORD’s word, you rulers of Sodom!
verses.11: “What are the multitude of your sacrifices to me?”, says the LORD.
verses.12: When you come to appear before me,
verses.14: My soul hates your New Moons and your appointed feasts.
verses.15: When you spread out your hands, I will hide my eyes from you.
verses.18: “Come now, and let’s reason together,” says the LORD:
verses.19: If you are willing and obedient,
verses.20: but if you refuse and rebel, you will be devoured with the sword;
verses.21: How the faithful city has become a prostitute!
verses.23: Your princes are rebellious and companions of thieves.
verses.24: Therefore the Lord, GOD of Armies,
verses.25: I will turn my hand on you,
verses.26: I will restore your judges as at the first,
verses.27: Zion shall be redeemed with justice,
verses.28: But the destruction of transgressors and sinners shall be together,
verses.29: For they shall be ashamed of the oaks which you have desired,
verses.30: For you shall be as an oak whose leaf fades,
verses.31: The strong will be like tinder,
